description_long: This is a large, modern commercial airplane with a wide fuselage and a rounded nose cone, featuring a prominent cockpit with blue-tinted windows. The aircraft has four turbofan engines mounted under its straight, low-set wings, which extend symmetrically from the fuselage. The wings are broad and lack visible winglets, while the tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled. The fuselage is primarily white with a yellow-tinted nose and tail tip, and rows of blue passenger windows line both sides. The overall design is streamlined for efficient flight, with smooth surfaces and minimal external protrusions.
description: A large, white commercial airplane with four underwing turbofan engines, blue cockpit windows, and a yellow-tinted nose and tail. It features straight, low-set wings and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, blue cockpit windows, and all four turbofan engines under the wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the row of blue passenger windows, wing profile, and yellow-tinted nose and tail tip.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the four engines.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite row of windows, wing, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broad wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with blue-tinted windows, likely multi-seat.
